summarization.a: The chart shows in situ FTIR difference spectra following consecutive TMGa-O3 exposures at 350 °C.

data_extraction.a:
| ALD Step | Wavenumber (cm⁻¹) |
|---|---|
| 1st TMGa | Trough at 1372, 1586, 3730; Peaks at 1215, 2915, 2975 |
| 1st Ozone | Troughs at 1215, 2915, 2975; Peaks at 1372, 1586, 3730 |
| 2nd TMGa | Trough at 1372, 1586, 3730; Peaks at 1215, 2915, 2975 |
| 2nd Ozone | Troughs at 1215, 2915, 2975; Peaks at 1372, 1586, 3730 |
| 3rd TMGa | Trough at 1372, 1586, 3730; Peaks at 1215, 2915, 2975 |
| 3rd Ozone | Troughs at 1215, 2915, 2975; Peaks at 1372, 1586, 3730 |

a.question: What was the operating temperature during the ALD experiment?
Where are the formate (F) vibrations typically observed?
How many ALD cycles are depicted here?
How do the FTIR difference spectra change over the ALD cycles?
a.answer: During TMGa exposure, an increase is observed at three positions and a decrease is observed at three other positions. During Ozone exposure, the exact opposite changes are observed. This is an indication that the same species desorb/react during each ALD cycle.
